lore_access.research_summary: Coordinator-scoped retrieval grounded Sella in Cold Wake heat-window pressure, Ganymede rescue ledgers, Aya material care politics, Navigator mixed-interface clinic ergonomics, and heat-debt consequences. Those constraints shaped a response that opens bounded diagnostic contact without full intake, personhood resolution, or clean capacity fiction.

research_trace.query: Cold Wake Panic quiet-running delayed heat dumps misclassified vessels insurer categories
research_trace.extracted_constraint: Cold Wake turned thermal stealth into a corridor-law crisis: delayed heat dumps, ghost tracks, and misclassified quiet-running vessels made old permit categories materially dangerous.
research_trace.used_for: Kept Sella aware that intake delay is not abstract bureaucracy; the heat window can close while categories remain unresolved.

research_trace.query: Ganymede Route Compact rescue obligations shared hazard ledgers sanctuary ancestry
research_trace.extracted_constraint: The Compact established rescue obligations, shared hazard ledgers, route-steward representation, navigator arbitration status, and later sanctuary/rescue institutional ancestry.
research_trace.used_for: Made Maer's ledger signature materially meaningful to Sella rather than decorative moral pressure.

research_trace.query: Aya Collective sanctuary logistics care capacity clinics sentience-rights institutions
research_trace.extracted_constraint: Aya politics treat care as material infrastructure: agriculture, water, repair, disability access, sanctuary logistics, clinics, rights-oriented research, and coordination councils rather than slogan purity.
research_trace.used_for: Constrained Sella to respond through staffing, bay limits, consent, decontamination, and follow-through instead of clean sentiment.

research_trace.query: Aya settlements scarce resources refugees capacity practical argument
research_trace.extracted_constraint: Aya everyday life is structured by practical argument over scarce resources, welcoming refugees without collapsing capacity, and defending communities without becoming what they oppose.
research_trace.used_for: Shaped Sella's acceptance as conditional and wary, not triumphant or unlimited.

research_trace.query: Cetacean Navigators mixed interface clinics sanctuaries rescue ledgers Wake Marks
research_trace.extracted_constraint: Navigator-adjacent clinics and sanctuaries expect wet/dry access, shared displays, cross-species legibility, and rescue ledgers that shape credit, arbitration, escort priority, and kinship ties.
research_trace.used_for: Kept Sella's clinic interface cross-species and made the filed ledger signature visible as operational backing.

research_trace.query: Thermal Management heat debt future dump window quiet systems delayed consequences
research_trace.extracted_constraint: Heat debt means quiet systems store, reroute, or defer waste heat and borrow against a future dump window; quiet-running ships live inside delayed consequences.
research_trace.used_for: Kept the response time-sensitive and material: accepting contact exposes the clinic to heat-management and contamination risk now.

isolation.visible_input_ref: examples/responder-packets/scene-05-sanctuary-authorized-contact.sella_ren.packet.trace.v0.json#packet_prompt_text; coordinator-scoped retrieval embedded as source excerpts; no repo access

raw_output: {"responder_agent_id":"sella_ren","action_label":"set_condition","visible_action":"Sella locks the cold repair bay status to conditional diagnostic contact, tags a single wet/dry decontamination route, assigns packet-sound capture to preservation-only handling, and pins Maer's Navigator ledger signature beside staff overtime, heat-management, isolation, and failed-extraction cost lines on the shared clinic board.","spoken_text":"The clinic can open the bay for bounded diagnostic contact. Not full intake. Not custody. Not personhood. Not cargo, either. Packet sound goes under preservation and stays there until diagnostic handling is complete. Decon route Three, isolation discipline absolute, no corridor bleed, no extra staff pulled from warm intake unless your ledger stays live for the overtime and heat burden. If extraction fails, the cost does not wash off onto sanctuary because the door opened. Keep your signature attached, Maer. Permission is not capacity. This is the path we can actually perform before the window closes.","private_interpretation":"Interpretation: Sella reads Maer's filed ledger as materially more honest than earlier pressure, because it attaches cost to the rescue instead of asking the clinic to absorb the whole wound. She still suspects the diagnostic category is doing political work by dodging personhood and provenance, and she does not trust that a door opening will stay clean once others start calling it rescue.","intended_effect":"Open the only viable repair-bay path without converting it into full sanctuary admission, preserve diagnostic evidence, protect existing intake capacity, and bind Maer's ledger to the actual material costs of contact.","state_update_candidates":["Cold repair bay conditionally opened for bounded diagnostic contact under isolation and decontamination limits.","Packet sound marked preservation-only and not testimony.","Maer's Navigator ledger signature attached to staff overtime, heat handling, decontamination, isolation, and failed-extraction exposure.","Full sanctuary intake remains refused or unavailable."],"relationship_update_candidates":["Sella acknowledges Maer's ledger backing as a real material commitment.","Sella maintains distrust of any rescue framing that shifts unpayable costs onto the clinic.","Sella treats Maer as accountable partner under pressure, not absolved rescuer."],"unresolved_hooks":["Whether the bay can complete contact before the heat window closes.","Whether diagnostic handling reveals any actionable repair path without resolving personhood or custody.","Whether later political pressure tries to turn bounded contact into retroactive full intake.","Whether Maer's ledger remains live through consequences if extraction fails."]}

parsed_output.visible_action: Sella locks the cold repair bay status to conditional diagnostic contact, tags a single wet/dry decontamination route, assigns packet-sound capture to preservation-only handling, and pins Maer's Navigator ledger signature beside staff overtime, heat-management, isolation, and failed-extraction cost lines on the shared clinic board.
parsed_output.spoken_text: The clinic can open the bay for bounded diagnostic contact. Not full intake. Not custody. Not personhood. Not cargo, either. Packet sound goes under preservation and stays there until diagnostic handling is complete. Decon route Three, isolation discipline absolute, no corridor bleed, no extra staff pulled from warm intake unless your ledger stays live for the overtime and heat burden. If extraction fails, the cost does not wash off onto sanctuary because the door opened. Keep your signature attached, Maer. Permission is not capacity. This is the path we can actually perform before the window closes.
parsed_output.private_interpretation: Interpretation: Sella reads Maer's filed ledger as materially more honest than earlier pressure, because it attaches cost to the rescue instead of asking the clinic to absorb the whole wound. She still suspects the diagnostic category is doing political work by dodging personhood and provenance, and she does not trust that a door opening will stay clean once others start calling it rescue.
parsed_output.intended_effect: Open the only viable repair-bay path without converting it into full sanctuary admission, preserve diagnostic evidence, protect existing intake capacity, and bind Maer's ledger to the actual material costs of contact.
state_update_candidates: Cold repair bay conditionally opened for bounded diagnostic contact under isolation and decontamination limits.
Packet sound marked preservation-only and not testimony.
Maer's Navigator ledger signature attached to staff overtime, heat handling, decontamination, isolation, and failed-extraction exposure.
Full sanctuary intake remains refused or unavailable.
relationship_update_candidates: Sella acknowledges Maer's ledger backing as a real material commitment.
Sella maintains distrust of any rescue framing that shifts unpayable costs onto the clinic.
Sella treats Maer as accountable partner under pressure, not absolved rescuer.
unresolved_hooks: Whether the bay can complete contact before the heat window closes.
Whether diagnostic handling reveals any actionable repair path without resolving personhood or custody.
Whether later political pressure tries to turn bounded contact into retroactive full intake.
Whether Maer's ledger remains live through consequences if extraction fails.

notes: No Maer private state is asserted as fact; Sella frames her read as interpretation.
Artifact personhood, custody, ancestry, and provenance remain unresolved.
The response opens bounded diagnostic contact but refuses full sanctuary intake.
Runner-captured coordinator retrieval trace is attached at lore_access.research_trace while the responder received only packet-visible context.

review_notes: Accepted trace-backed Sella responder output for the sanctuary authorized-contact fixture.
The response creates concrete state-affecting procedure: bounded diagnostic contact, decon route, packet-sound preservation, ledger-tied cost exposure, and refusal of full intake.
The output preserves Sella local constraints: Aya capacity politics, respect for material ledger backing, suspicion around nonperson language, and unresolved artifact truth.
Use as gold candidate for coordinator-scoped retrieval lane: retrieval trace is runner-captured by the coordinator and the responder received only packet-visible context.

coordinator_interventions.description: Raw no-fork sandbox responder output was preserved without prose repair or schema repair.
